title: A Woman Becomes the Top Leader of the Church of England
summary: Dame Sarah Mullally is becoming the first female Archbishop of Canterbury, which is a big job leading the Church of England. She will have a special ceremony at Canterbury Cathedral. Many important people, like the Prince and Princess of Wales, will be there to watch. Dame Sarah used to be a nurse, and she invited nurses and carers to the event. This is a historic moment because all the previous archbishops were men. Women were only allowed to become priests in the Church of England starting in 1994. Dame Sarah says she wants to celebrate women and show others they can follow their dreams too. Before the ceremony, she walked a long way on a pilgrimage to the cathedral.

a person who helps sick people, like Dame Sarah used to be

background_read: The Church of England is a Christian church in England. For a long time, only men could be priests or leaders in the church. In 1994, women were allowed to become priests. Now, Dame Sarah Mullally is making history as the first female Archbishop of Canterbury, showing that women can hold top roles too. She was a nurse before, helping people, and she wants to inspire others to follow their dreams.
Article_Structure: WHO: Dame Sarah Mullally, the first female Archbishop of Canterbury
WHAT: A ceremony to install her as the leader of the Church of England
WHY: To mark a historic moment and celebrate women's roles in the church

perspectives.description: Dame Sarah sees this as a way to honor women and show they can achieve their dreams.
